title: Level 595
wikitext:
{{Notice|body=I am open to suggestions. Feel free to leave yours. - AA}}
You
find
yourself
in
a
strange
place
{{Class 0|entity=Low Entity Count|color-h=255158228|hexagon=⊞}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=95shutdown.png|caption=𝕎𝕚𝕟𝕕𝕠𝕨𝕤 𝕚𝕤 𝕤𝕙𝕦𝕥𝕥𝕚𝕟𝕘 𝕕𝕠𝕨𝕟...}}{{Audio|file=NewVapor.mp3|volume=1}} Level 595 is a boundless assortment of areas that heavily takes on the Windows XP and Vaporwave aesthetic. Certain kinds of music play throughout the level, commonly in the "Vaporwave" genre. Most structures in this level are shown to have extremely smooth architecture, some describing it as '''perfectly''' smooth, despite this being scientifically impossible. The appearance of the sky seems to vary based on events and time in the level. Sometimes, the sky will appear to "shut down" and turn completely black, which strangely does not seem to affect the lighting of Level 595. Further investigation has also shown there are different "zones" within the level.ㅤ ㅤ |-| Paradise= {{Blurimage|file=Pink.webp|caption=Ｐａｒａｄｉｓｅ}} '''Paradise''' is the main area of Level 595, and where one will find themselves upon entering the level. This zone seems to have the smoothest architecture of all, and seems to bring the sensation of comfort and relaxation to those who enter. One may encounter a phenomenon known as 'New Windows', which seem to display applications or notifications from Windows 95. This may display one of the following: *Microsoft Paint *"System Errors" *Notifications asking if you want to leave the level *Search Browsers This phenomenon does not seem to pose a threat to Wanderers or Entities within the level. While they may foreshadow events in the level, there is no evidence that the Windows cause the events to happen, or even have anything to do with the events. |-| The Mall= {{Blurimage|file=VaporMall.jpg|caption=Ｔｈｅ　Ｍａｌｌ}} '''The Mall''' is an uncommon area in Level 595. Items in this zone are more common than any other zones, and are free to take from empty shops as there is no one around to stop anyone from doing so. Some desktops can be found around the area, however all reported laptops are Windows 95. They seem to run very well despite their age. Attempting to run any other operating system will not work and may cause a system restart in the level. While Wanderers are not common in this zone, it is more likely one will encounter a Glowfolk in this area, as they commonly say this is the first area they end up in. They are rarely encountered, however they are often seen running shops and selling very unique items, mostly objects that do not appear in this level. |-| The Control Room= {{Blurimage|file=Takecontrol.png|caption=Ｔａｋｅ　Ｃｏｎｔｒｏｌ}} '''The Control Room''' is the rarest area in Level 595. The Control Room is a singular room that can be accessed by noclipping into Notification Windows, which has a larger chance to send you to the Void. From here, one is able to have limited control over Level 595. This includes but is not limited to: *Changing the Sky *Summoning "New Windows" *Causing a System Restart *Entering a numbered level of one's choice While the Control Room does not offer unlimited control of the level, and does not serve as an exit from the Backrooms, it is a good way to help others within the level. Even so, attempting entry is not advised, as all methods of entering are very dangerous. |-| Blue Death= {{Blurimage|file=You went too far.jpg|caption=Ｔｏｏ　Ｆａｒ}} '''Blue Death''' is the final zone of Level 595, and also the most dangerous zone in the level. Blue Death is a rumor, but is said to be encountered when one goes "out of bounds" in Level 595, which is theoretically up to 24000 miles away. While it has no entities, those who enter will meet an unknown fate as they go farther into the forest. Some say the victim's body will deteriorate or freeze, while others believe their soul becomes a part of this zone. The Sky in this zone is said to take the appearance of a "Blue Screen of Death" that appears on the Windows Operating Systems, however, this has not yet been proven. The temperature is also said to remain at a constant 0 degrees or lower. Not much more is known about this zone, however it is highly unadvised to attempt entry. ==Entities== One may rarely encounter a [[Glowfolk]] while traveling throughout the level. They are easy to spot, as their colors become more vibrant upon entry of the level. However, despite being the only discovered entity on the level, all reported Glowfolk that enter the level claim to be lost. Lost Glowfolk will likely act confused and scared, typically running away if they are below the age of Infernos, who are more likely to behave normally and ask for directions. Frightened Infernos, however, are more likely willing to fight. If a Glowfolk is encountered, approach slowly with caution, and do not make any sudden movements. ==Events== ===System Restart=== This event is rare, but has been reported to cause the level to go completely dark and silent. A Notification Window will inform any confused Wanderers of a 'System Restart'. Speculation and research has led to believe that this happens whenever something goes wrong in the level, such as the entry of a hostile entity, or if something is "glitched" in the level for too long. Once Level 595 undergoes a restart, any problem that may have existed before then will be fixed, and the level will go back to normal. ===Glitching=== Glitching is a rare phenomenon that affects inanimate objects, humans and entities, causing them to act in a strange manner. Some have reported glitched objects stuck in walls, pixelated, or taking a low poly form. It is advised not to approach or interact with glitched objects, as it will likely cause one to get the same effect and cause a system restart, which has a chance to fix or delete a glitched person or entity. While it is still unknown what happens if one is deleted, many speculate it sends one to the [[The Void|Void]]. Though the chance of being deleted is low, it is still advised to stay away from glitched objects. If a glitched object exists for too long, a system restart will occur and fix the problem. == Discovery Log == === Author Unknown === Things were looking bad for me. I was stuck in Level 9. If I had spent any longer there, I would've gone mad. But just as I felt as if I was going to black out, I saw a computer screen. I hadn't seen any other working electronics in this place, so I felt a bit relieved when I saw the screen turn on out of nowhere. As I booted it up, I found out it was just an old Windows 95 computer. Kind of a disappointment, but right then, I found myself being dragged in. Now I'm here, in this serene place. I don't know what it is, but I just felt at peace the instant I heard the sound of the music playing. Now here I am, unsure of what to do next. But I have to leave somehow, some way. No matter how peaceful this place is, the Backrooms is infinite, and I know that if I keep trying, I'll find a way out. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * Finding a rare working Windows 95 computer in [[Level 9]]. * Start up a Windows 95 computer in [[Level 514]]. * 'New Windows' asking if one wants to go here can appear in levels connected to the Neon Line in [[Level 154]]. === Exits === * Clicking "Yes" on a Notification Window asking "Do you want to leave" will send one to [[Level 4]]. * Noclipping during a System Restart will send one to the [[Level 111|Malfunctional Metropolis]]. * Press Control + Alt + Delete on any laptops may send one to [[The End]]. * Holding Control + Alt + Shift and pressing tech support on Microsoft Excel will send one to [[Level 195]]. * Escaping Blue Death will send one to the [[The Blue Space|Blue Space]].
